title: China's UN Ambassador Signals Strait of Hormuz Will Dominate Trump-Xi Summit Agenda
summary: China's UN Ambassador Fu Cong stated that the Strait of Hormuz closure will rank high on the summit agenda when Trump visits Beijing, underscoring the global energy crisis dimension of the US-Iran war.
contents:
## China Elevates Hormuz Issue to Summit Priority

### UN Statement
China's UN Ambassador Fu Cong told reporters on Friday that the Strait of Hormuz issue would certainly feature prominently on the agenda if the waterway remains closed when President Trump visits China this month for the planned May 14-15 summit.

### Energy Security Dimension
The Strait of Hormuz closure has disrupted approximately 20% of global oil and gas supplies, with Brent crude hovering above $109 per barrel. China, as a major energy importer, faces direct economic consequences from the blockade.

### Ceasefire Urgency
Fu Cong described maintaining the current US-Iran ceasefire as "an urgent necessity" and called for the Strait to reopen "as quickly as possible." His statements reflect Beijing's growing frustration with the conflict's economic spillover effects.

### Diplomatic Positioning
China has positioned itself as an interested mediator, with separate high-level calls between Wang Yi and Rubio, and between Vice Premier He Lifeng and Treasury Secretary Bessent. The economic and trade consultation mechanism between both nations continues operating despite tensions.

### Global Impact
India has already raised cooking gas and jet fuel prices due to supply disruptions, and multiple nations report economic strain from elevated energy costs linked to the Hormuz closure.
